programmes.show_description: Blood: In pursuit of Michelin stars, Manny and Bernard transform the infested bookshop into a restaurant. Meanwhile, Fran explores her family tree.
Best bits from the last series of 8 Out of 10 Cats, with Jimmy Carr, Rob Beckett, Katherine Ryan, Tom Allen, Rosie Jones, Phil Wang, Gemma Collins, Richard Osman and many more.
An extended edition of the panel show with host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson, plus guests Hannibal Buress, Sam Buttery, Stephen Mangan and Katherine Ryan.
An extended edition with host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson, who are joined by guests David Hasselhoff, Rachel Riley, Rob Beckett and Justin Moorhouse.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Anthony Wedo, the president and CEO of buffet restaurant organisation Buffets, Inc., goes undercover among his workforce. Can he turn the firm around after years of challenges?
Amit Kleinberger, CEO of frozen yogurt chain Menchie's, goes undercover to work out what changes must be made at grassroots level to make the company a billion-dollar business.
In Luton, there's unpalatable behaviour, curdled ice cream, an eggy mishap and a hair-raising confession, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
In Devon, Matt and Sarah admit to not knowing what scallops or celeriac are, Jake and Ian question the canapés (crisps), and foodies James and Laura get competitive with a French theme.
...Selling: Francis, Juanita and their kids still love their home but they've outgrown it. Can Jonathan make it look brand-spanking new while Drew searches for a high-class house?
The One with the Helicopter: Reece, who is ex-military, plans a wedding with an army twist. But his bride Lucy would prefer something in a castle.
The Adventure Sports One: Excitable groom Jonny plans an adrenaline-fuelled wedding for his bride-to-be Kate, who favours something simple.
Undercover bosses Amit Kleinberger, Randy DeWitt and Steve Greenbaum take another look inside their firms, but this time they each send an employee undercover in their place.
Jeff Platt, the president and CEO of a chain of indoor trampoline parks, goes undercover in his own company, to find out whether his fast-growing business is running smoothly.
Lakeview Hotel: Gordon Ramsay confronts the owner of the Lakeview Hotel on Lake Chelan in Washington. The bedrooms are empty and the restaurant and ice cream parlour are failing.
Brick Hotel: Can Gordon convince the owner of the historic Brick Hotel in Newtown, Pennsylvania, to value her loyal workers and entice locals back to this landmark establishment?
...and Selling: Brand new series: Mike, Steve and their four children have outgrown their cosy cottage. Will a 200-year-old property be what they're looking for?
Much Ado About Crushing: The fight for Chase's affection heats up. And the drama bubbles over as a wild night out in a hot tub ends with an unexpected bang.
The contestants argue about facial hair. Mike Wozniak vaults a high fence. And Lee Mack struggles to see a balloon.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Miles Jupp take on Jon Richardson and Rose Matafeo. Vic Reeves joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Victoria Coren Mitchell take on Jon Richardson and James Acaster. Morgana Robinson joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jeremy Makes It: Jeremy meets an old classmate, Gog, who he used to bully. Gog is now successful and invites Jeremy to work on his latest art house movie.
Local Zero: Jeff is going on a work trip with Sophie to Aberdeen, and Mark - fearful they will sleep together - becomes desperate to stop them...
Harry wants a memorial tattoo dedicated to a very intimate part of himself after surviving testicular cancer. Nicole has a portrait that resembles armpit hair.
The contestants argue about facial hair. Mike Wozniak vaults a high fence. And Lee Mack struggles to see a balloon.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Miles Jupp take on Jon Richardson and Rose Matafeo. Vic Reeves joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Paul Quentel, the president of Alfred Angelo, the second-largest bridal retailer in the USA, poses as a reality TV contestant to go undercover in his own company.
Mike Bloom, the president and COO of discount chain Family Dollar, goes incognito to find out where the firm is excelling and where it needs improvement.
In Devon, there's tension at Frankie and Jason's table, Kate and Ian entertain their guests with a bird of prey, and a surprise discovery could make Chris and Amy's meal hard to stomach...
In Leicester, there's food inspired by luxury cruises, a moving musical performance that reduces one guest to tears, some outspoken comments and a serving of army-style 'banter'.
...Selling: Firefighter Ryan and Lesley are ready to set their property apart. But as Jonathan tackles turf trouble, will Drew be too busy with a firefighter fitness challenge?
The One with the Leak: Yanis wants his big day to be a towering success, featuring the Eiffel Tower. But when the wedding plans are leaked, the couple are left facing the consequences.
The One at a Rugby Match: Rugby-loving Wes plans the biggest wedding ever, in front of 26,000 people at a rugby match. But bride-to-be Charlotte wants something intimate.
John Hartmann, a former FBI agent and the president and CEO of Chicago-based hardware co-operative True Value, goes incognito among his workforce.
Bill Peduto, the 60th mayor of Pittsburgh, goes incognito among his workforce, to hear from the city's employees and ensure that the changes he's introduced have been effective.
Beachfront Inn & Inlet: Gordon Ramsay visits a 'Caribbean-style' resort hotel in Fort Pierce, Florida, where guests face dusty rooms, late-night concerts and poor service.
Landoll's Mohican Castle: Gordon Ramsay visits a custom-built, multi-million-dollar hotel in Loudonville, Ohio. Can he help the struggling business and its overworked owner?
...and Selling: Brand new series: Jeremy and Dayna need some real estate therapy. As the brothers dish out the diagnoses, Dayna realises she might not be ready to move.
Fraught Mess: A group of friends hop onboard looking for luxury. But will they get it? Chef Cloyce struggles with a seven-course dinner and difficult diners.
Lee Mack attempts an audacious hammer throw, Sarah Kendall makes sitting down memorable, and Jamali Maddix forgets his passport.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson take on Jon Richardson and Sara Pascoe. Sam Simmons joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Rob Beckett take on guest team captain Alan Carr and Sarah Millican. Nick Mohammed joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
University Challenge: Mark blurs the line between enthusiastic new boyfriend and stalker. Jeremy joins a new band, but insists that he is neither a roadie nor a Bez.
The Man Show: Mark becomes depressed over Sophie living with Jeff, but Jeremy is not sure that self-mutilation is the answer. Besides, Jeremy and Jeff are getting on really well.
